description_long: This object is a cluster of oyster mushrooms, featuring multiple overlapping, fan-shaped caps with a smooth, slightly matte surface. The caps are pale beige to light brown, with subtle variations in tone and a gentle, undulating margin. The stems are short, thick, and creamy white, converging at a common base, and the gills are visible beneath the caps, running down the stems in a delicate, radial pattern. The overall form is organic and layered, with the caps fanning outward in a natural, irregular arrangement. The surface of the caps appears soft and slightly velvety, with no prominent spots or scales. The cluster is compact and dense, resembling mushrooms that might be found growing on decaying wood in shaded, moist environments.
description: A dense cluster of pale beige oyster mushrooms with smooth, fan-shaped caps and visible gills, arranged in overlapping layers. The stems are thick and creamy, converging at a common base.
description_view_0: A side view showing the overlapping, fan-shaped caps and thick stems, with some caps casting shadows on others.
description_view_1: A top-down view highlighting the broad, smooth caps and the radial arrangement of the cluster.
description_view_2: A side angle emphasizing the layered structure and the curvature of the caps.
description_view_3: A rear view displaying the convergence of the thick stems and the underside of the caps with visible gills.
